status: Fixed in taskflow close atomicity lane
area: Taskflow close / planning mirror index cleanup
finding: `taskflow close --write` can report a successful planning mirror commit but leave the planning repo index staged with the inverse closeback diff, making the card look reopened/deleted in `git status` even though the just-created commit contains the correct done frontmatter and close event.
expectedBehavior: A successful taskflow close transaction should leave both target and planning repos with no task-owned staged residue; if cleanup cannot complete, the command should fail closed or emit an explicit recovery command before reporting the transaction committed.
evidenceOrRepro: During `TASK-AAO-0150`, close write created ATM close commit `1a9683e2` and 3KLife planning commit `cd57450b`, but `git -C C:\Users\User\3KLife status --short` then showed staged deletion of `.atm/history/task-events/TASK-AAO-0150/...close...json` and mixed `TASK-AAO-0150-next-hot-path-latency.task.md`. The source fix in `commit-bundle-assembly.ts` commits taskflow bundles from a temporary index and then resets only task-owned stage files in the live index to the newly committed HEAD tree, preserving unrelated staged files while removing stale inverse diffs.
followUp: Keep `packages/cli/src/commands/taskflow/__tests__/commit-bundle-assembly.spec.ts` and `validate-taskflow-close-atomicity` coverage for post-commit live-index residue cleanup in both target and planning close bundles.
